findingDetails.title: model-benchmark route never invokes the generic model dispatcher
Direct dispatcher runs ignore the requested working directory for most executors
D3 cwd checker can miss traversal into sibling paths with the same prefix

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: 29-file curated scope with new benchmark-mode orchestration, dispatcher, and scorer code

targetSelection.selectionReason: D1 correctness focus targeted mode routing, dispatcher args/cwd behavior, scorer invariants, and test/spec evidence

searchLedger.hypothesis: model-benchmark can produce benchmark records without running any model executor

searchActions.result: model-benchmark plan contains materialize and run-benchmark only
no dispatcher wiring found in loop-host/run-benchmark/tests

searchLedger.rationale: central model-benchmark route skips the dispatcher required by spec

searchLedger.hypothesis: per-executor dispatch can run from the wrong directory even when opts.cwd is set

searchActions.result: dir is computed but spawnSync has no cwd and most executor specs do not consume dir

searchLedger.rationale: cross-runtime executor map does not honor the advertised cwd contract

searchLedger.invariant: Traversal resolving outside fixture cwd must not be classified as bare_relative

searchActions.result: containment uses startsWith on absolute strings

searchLedger.rationale: prefix collision permits sibling-directory traversal false negatives

searchLedger.invariant: unknown mode falls back to agent-improvement

searchActions.result: resolveMode warns and returns agent-improvement for unknown modes
test covers unknown-mode fallback

searchLedger.rationale: fallback behavior is implemented and tested
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by direct read of implementation and test

searchActions.result: plan orders materialize before run-benchmark and aborts on failed steps
test asserts EC-5 ordering

searchLedger.rationale: ordering requirement is satisfied
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by plan and unit test

searchLedger.invariant: D2 hard gate caps D1 and weighted score uses declared rubric dimensions

searchActions.result: hard gate caps D1 and weighted score sums rubric weights over D1-D5

searchLedger.rationale: no arithmetic defect found in this pass
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by direct read of hard-gate and weighted-score logic

findingDetails.title: Benchmark criteria can execute arbitrary shell commands and can escape the scorer cwd
findingDetails.claim: criteria.acceptance commands and files are trusted as data but executed or resolved without allowlisting/containment
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: checked dispatch-model CLI spawning; prompt/model/agent use argv arrays and stdin rather than shell strings
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: fixtures may be trusted local repo data only, limiting exploitability but not enforced by the scorer API
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: trusted, pinned fixture provenance plus no user-provided criteria path

findingDetails.title: D4 grader accepts unbounded model-provided scores and can poison benchmark integrity
findingDetails.claim: untrusted grader output is parsed without range/schema validation and consumed directly in weighted scoring/cache
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: mock grader outputs are in range, but no real-output validation or clamp exists before scoring
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: operator-controlled grader model reduces external attacker exposure but leaves scoring integrity vulnerable to malformed model output
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: strict caller-side schema validation before score consumption or permanent disabling of real grader mode

findingDetails.title: Grader cache persists raw model output that may echo sensitive candidate content

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: 29-file curated scope with spawned CLIs, scorer subprocesses, grader parsing, and cache persistence

targetSelection.selectionReason: D2 security focus targeted subprocess creation, criteria-driven command execution, path containment, grader output parsing, and cache persistence

searchLedger.invariant: prompt/model/agent must not be concatenated into a shell command

searchActions.result: executor specs build argv arrays and spawnSync receives spec.bin/spec.args; codex prompt uses stdin

searchLedger.rationale: no shell-string execution in the primary CLI dispatcher path
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by direct read of buildSpawnSpec and spawnSync call

searchLedger.hypothesis: benchmark criteria can inject shell commands through deterministic or smoke-run acceptance

searchActions.result: deterministic acceptance executes a.command with execSync
bundle gate executes acceptance.command with execSync

searchLedger.rationale: criteria strings cross from data into shell execution

searchLedger.hypothesis: accepted absolute cwd plus unchecked criterion file paths can read/probe outside the fixture root

searchActions.result: cwd only checked for absolute; grep paths join cwd with user-supplied file without containment check

searchLedger.rationale: same criteria trust boundary permits path escape in addition to command execution

searchLedger.hypothesis: model-produced grader JSON can carry out-of-range scores into benchmark scoring

searchActions.result: strict/fenced/regex/score-only parser paths accept numeric score without range validation
grader.score is consumed directly as D4 and weighted

searchLedger.rationale: unvalidated model output can poison evaluation integrity

searchLedger.hypothesis: raw grader output can persist echoed sensitive candidate content in cache blobs

searchActions.result: raw_grader_output is included in result and cached blob body
cache blob persists metadata header plus output text; keys are hashes rather than raw secrets

searchLedger.rationale: not a direct key leak, but raw persisted grader text is a retention risk

searchLedger.hypothesis: spawned model CLIs inherit the full process environment

searchActions.result: spawnSync does not pass an env override, so Node inherits process.env by default
execFileSync likewise omits env override

searchLedger.rationale: CLIs may require credential environment variables; severity needs an explicit runtime secret-policy target
searchLedger.deferredReason: needs policy decision on minimal environment allowlist for model executors

findingDetails.title: Benchmark criteria can execute arbitrary shell commands and can escape the scorer cwd (CONFIRMED UNCHANGED)
findingDetails.claim: string execSync without allowlisting or argv splitting

findingDetails.title: D4 grader accepts unbounded model-provided scores (CONFIRMED UNCHANGED)
findingDetails.claim: unbounded numeric score via parseFloat regex fallback
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: caller-side schema validation or permanent disabling of real grader mode

findingDetails.title: dispute.cjs uses global fs monkey-patch for adversarial grader call
findingDetails.claim: fs.readFileSync is globally patched to swap system-grader.md for system-skeptic.md; contained by try-finally but is a code smell

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: D2 second pass targeting grader dispatch, cache writes, and MiniMax skill docs

targetSelection.selectionReason: D2 second pass focusing on grader harness dispatchReal, cache file writes, and cross-checking iteration 3 findings

searchLedger.hypothesis: grader harness dispatchReal may use unsafe shell execution

searchActions.result: execFileSync with argv array, not shell string

searchLedger.rationale: execFileSync with argv array is safe

searchLedger.hypothesis: cache.cjs may use unsafe temp file patterns

searchActions.result: mkdtempSync + rename pattern is atomic and safe

searchLedger.rationale: atomic write via temp+rename confirmed

searchLedger.hypothesis: dispute.cjs monkey-patch could escape scope

searchActions.result: global fs.readFileSync patch; contained by try-finally

searchLedger.rationale: contained but is a code smell; not a security exploit in current form

findingDetails.title: model-benchmark runner bypasses the decoupled 5-dim scorer required by REQ-004
findingDetails.claim: REQ-004 and the 002 build-delta require the model-benchmark scorer seam to use primitive criteria arrays and the ported 5-dim scorer, but the active benchmark runner still scores fixture headings/patterns directly and never imports score-model-variant.cjs.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched for score-model-variant/buildGraderFn imports and calls across scripts; hits are the scorer itself plus tests/CLI, not run-benchmark or loop-host.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: spec.md:166 says activating the 5-dim scorer by default may be a follow-on, but that contradicts REQ-004 and the in-scope deliverables unless it is an approved deferral.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Document an approved deferral that removes active 5-dim scorer use from REQ-004, or wire run-benchmark through score-model-variant.cjs behind an explicit scorer seam.

findingDetails.title: promotion still has only the scored path despite the spec claiming model-benchmark promotion is resolved
findingDetails.claim: REQ-002 and the 003 open-question note say model-benchmark promotion returns a decision and routes by scored vs benchmark-complete status, but promote-candidate.cjs rejects any score whose status is not scored before it checks the benchmark report.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked parseArgs for --mode, the early score.status gate, benchmarkReport.status checks, and loop-host steps; no model-benchmark promotion branch or loop-host promotion invocation exists.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The code may intend benchmark reports to be an ancillary gate for agent-candidate promotion, but then REQ-002/spec.md:165 overclaim model-benchmark promotion behavior.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Clarify the spec that promotion remains agent-candidate only, or add a model-benchmark branch that accepts benchmark-complete input and returns the documented decision.

findingDetails.title: deterministic checks still depend on fixture JSON instead of the explicit --cwd contract
findingDetails.claim: REQ-004 and research CP2 require deterministic check scripts to accept explicit --cwd, but the shipped det-check CLIs still accept fixturePath/outputPath and recover cwd from fixture.scope.cwd.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched deterministic scripts for --cwd parsing; no --cwd support exists, and score-model-variant.cjs writes a temporary virtual fixture to preserve the old fixturePath API.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The temporary virtual fixture with an absolute cwd reduces coupling at the wrapper boundary, but it does not meet the stated det-check CLI acceptance criterion.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Change REQ-004 to accept the virtual-fixture adapter as the intended contract, or add explicit --cwd support to the deterministic check scripts.

rate-limit backoff is a synchronous busy wait, making dispatch hard to test and expensive to operate

findingDetails.title: Reducer/dashboard do not surface the new mode metadata required by the mode-field integration
findingDetails.claim: The record writers add mode, but the reducer/dashboard integration still does not pass mode into reduced metadata or display it, despite the build spec making reduce-state.cjs mode metadata pass-through and dashboard display an in-scope requirement.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the reducer for record.mode, mode:, benchmark_run, dashboard rendering, and metadata references. The raw record is preserved as latestRecord/benchmarkRuns, but no reducer-level mode metadata or dashboard display was found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The implementation may treat preserving raw records as sufficient pass-through, but that does not meet the explicit dashboard-display contract.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the spec is amended to require mode only on raw JSONL records, not reduced metadata or dashboard display.
